title: aulacocalyx diervilleoides
intro: Aulacocalyx diervilleoides is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to tropical Africa. It is known to possess medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues. Aulacocalyx diervilleoides is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The plant's bark is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it may also be used in the form of a tincture or ointment, although further research is necessary to confirm its efficacy and optimal usage.
